content.title: IBM Cybersecurity Analyst Professional Certificate
content.summary: The IBM Cybersecurity Analyst Professional Certificate is a beginner-friendly program covering security foundations, operating systems, networks, databases, architecture, compliance, incident response, penetration testing, AI in security, and career preparation. It combines theory with labs and tool-based practice.

credential_details.level: beginner with intermediate tool and framework exposure

knowledge_areas: Core cybersecurity principles, including common threats, authentication, encryption, and defense in depth.
Operating system administration and security across Windows, Linux, and macOS.
Database essentials, vulnerabilities, and SQL-related risk.
Compliance frameworks, standards, regulations, and audit concepts.
Penetration testing, threat hunting, and cryptography.
Case-study analysis and capstone-style breach review.
learning_outcomes: Understand and apply widely used security principles and frameworks across operations, architecture, and governance.
Configure and harden systems and networks against common threats.
Detect, investigate, and respond to incidents using structured workflows and real tools.
Perform or support vulnerability assessment, penetration testing, and forensic analysis tasks.
Connect technical controls to compliance, audit, and business-risk requirements.
tools_and_frameworks: Tools and lab environments: IBM QRadar, OWASP ZAP, Linux administration labs, network simulation labs, SQL injection labs, and forensics workflows.
Core frameworks and concepts: CIA triad, NIST CSF, MITRE ATT&CK, CIS Controls v8, Zero Trust, ISO 27001, NIST RMF, GDPR, PCI DSS, COBIT, and NIST AI RMF.
Output types mentioned in the source: audit templates, risk registers, secure architecture blueprints, penetration-test reports, forensic investigation logs, and AI-assisted threat-hunting workflows.

proof.label: Credly badge: IBM Cybersecurity Analyst Professional Certificate
